etymology.description: 衤(衣) ('clothes') + 刀(刀) ('knife; to cut') = starting to make clothes by cutting the cloth
etymology.interpretation: This character combines elements representing 'clothes' and 'knife' to convey the concept of beginning the process of making clothes by cutting the cloth, symbolizing the start or initial phase.

examples.chinese: 我们在新年的开始(我們在新年的開始)，总是感到充满希望(總是感到充滿希望)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài xīnnián de kāishǐ, zǒng shì gǎndào chōngmǎn xīwàng.
examples.english: At the beginning of the new year, we always feel full of hope.

examples.chinese: 春天初到时(春天初到時)，天气总是很宜人(天氣總是很宜人)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān chū dào shí, tiānqì zǒng shì hěn yírén.
examples.english: When spring initially arrives, the weather is always pleasant.

examples.chinese: 在学习初期(在學習初期)，很多人会感到有点困难(很多人會感到有點困難)。
examples.pinyin: Zài xuéxí chūqī, hěn duō rén huì gǎndào yǒudiǎn kùnnán.
examples.english: In the initial stages of learning, many people feel a bit challenged.
